title: DeckJSON
description: Single-source-of-truth data model for a feishu-deck-h5 slide deck. 14 base layouts (content/stats/flow multi-variant) + 3 specials (replica/raw/canvas). The deterministic renderer turns this into HTML; the visual editor edits this in place; the LLM produces this instead of free-form HTML.

version.description: DeckJSON schema version. Renderers MUST refuse unknown versions.

assets.description: Optional asset manifest. Renderer can resolve relative paths against this; if absent, slide-level paths are used as-is.

base_dir.description: Filesystem dir to resolve relative paths from.

notes.description: Free-form deck-level authoring notes. Not rendered.

title.description: Deck title. Used in <title> and as fallback for cover-slide title.

deck_id.description: Stable per-deck identity, auto-assigned by render-deck.py on first render and persisted here. Stamped as data-deck-id on <div class="deck">; the in-browser edit mode compares it to refuse overwriting a DIFFERENT deck's index.html (every deck is named index.html). Do not hand-edit or copy between decks.

author.description: Initiator's personal name. Used on cover when slide doesn't override.

presentation_date.description: ISO YYYY-MM-DD. Used for delivery filename (lark-<customer>-<date>.html).

customer_slug.description: Lowercase kebab-case. Used for delivery filename + library deck_id.

language.description: ZH-only is the default (LANGUAGE POLICY). Bilingual is opt-in.

mode.description: rewrite = full HTML composition via layouts; replica = each slide is a page-as-image.

title_style.description: Deck-wide default for content / stats / flow / etc. titles. Mirrors PPT master '内容页-标题…' variants. Per-slide overrides via slide.title_style.

logo_position.description: Deck-wide default for customer × feishu logo arrangement on content slides. 'front' = customer logo before feishu; 'back' = after. Mirrors PPT '内容页-飞书联名在前/后'.

magic_move.description: OPT-IN · Keynote-style Magic Move on page turn via the View Transitions API. When true, the renderer emits data-magic-move on the deck root and feishu-deck.js wraps each present-mode slide change in document.startViewTransition() (feature-detected; gracefully instant on Firefox; gated by prefers-reduced-motion). Off by default so the fs-reveal entrance baseline is unchanged. To make an element MORPH between two pages, give the same `view-transition-name` to its counterpart on both slides via slide.custom_css (round-trips). See references/motion-system.md §7.

hide_progress.description: OPT-IN · Hide the thin top present-mode reading-progress keyline (.deck-progress, a 3px brand-gradient bar) deck-wide, WITHOUT needing the per-URL #clean / #kiosk hash (those also hide the bottom controls + nav hints). Renderer emits data-hide-progress on the deck root; CSS hides only the progress bar (controls/hints stay). Use for formal client/exec decks where that top gradient line reads as an unwanted top border. Default false.

motion_engine.description: OPT-IN · Entrance animation engine. 'css' (default) = built-in fs-reveal CSS stagger: zero-dependency, validator-introspectable, the historical baseline (absent or 'css' → byte-for-byte the CSS baseline, fully non-breaking). 'gsap' = renderer vendors GSAP (assets/gsap/*) + loads feishu-deck-motion.js, which replaces fs-reveal with a choreographed per-slide timeline (title word-rise + depth stagger + SVG draw-on + number count-up; cover/section/closing get a cinematic char reveal). The engine NEVER pre-hides content (resting state stays visible) so a failed/late GSAP load degrades to 'no animation', never to lost content. Trade-offs vs 'css': +~88KB vendored JS per deck, and the CSSOM-based DEAD-ANIM/DEAD-RULE audits cannot introspect JS-driven motion (the visible-resting guarantee is the safety net). Bespoke per-page choreography (Flip/MorphSVG/Draggable) is NOT covered — use the iframe escape hatch for that. See references/motion-system.md §8. Default 'css'.

gate.description: F-292 · Delivery-gate strictness for the error-level VISUAL audits (the font-size/floor/tier/hier class promoted to a hard BLOCK by F-256). 'block' (default, for NEW decks) = an error-level visual defect on a real (runs/) render BLOCKS delivery (return 4). 'advisory' = those visual errors are PRINTED but do NOT block — for STOCK / IMPORTED decks carrying historical violations, so a first re-render isn't walled by pre-existing nits with no edit in between. NOTE: 'advisory' only relaxes the visual class; HARD GEOMETRY (overflow / overlap / card-overflow / band-collide) and the static validator gate stay full-block regardless (overflow/overlap is the most plainly user-visible breakage and is almost never intentional). An imported deck (every slide lifted, or <meta fs-deck-origin=imported>) is treated as 'advisory' automatically even without this field.

style_contract.description: OPTIONAL · raw-first. The deck's OWN design decisions, declared ONCE so every page stays consistent with them and the deck-level consistency audits (R-DECK-*) can check against intent instead of inferring the mode. This does NOT dictate design — the model chooses freely; it only records the choice so pages don't each re-invent it. Omit it and the consistency audits fall back to statistical mode detection. Round-trips with the deck.

accent_plan.description: How accent colors are assigned across the deck, e.g. 'blue throughout; section dividers teal'.

card_style.description: The deck's card/panel convention: radius / border / background tint, e.g. 'radius 16px · 1px rgba border · no fill'.

title_baseline.description: Where content-page titles sit, e.g. 'raw master coords top:61 left:73'.

motion.description: The deck's bespoke motion language, if any, so later edits keep it consistent.

notes.description: Any other deck-wide design constant a page should honor.

key.description: Deck-internal unique semantic slug. NOT positional. Stable across reorders. (R-KEY)

variant.description: Required for multi-variant layouts (content / stats / flow). Ignored for single-variant layouts. Schema enforces validity per-layout.

screen_label.description: Pager UI label (data-screen-label). If omitted, derived from title.

accent.description: Slide accent color. Per R49, cyan is NOT allowed at slide level.

decor.description: Atmospheric decoration tokens. Space-stacked at render time.

allow.description: Slide-level visual-audit opt-outs, emitted as `data-allow-<token>` on the `.slide` element. The ONLY authoring channel for the slide-scoped opt-outs the visual engine checks via `slide.hasAttribute`: `imbalance` (R-VIS-BALANCE / CROWD / PANEL-TOP / CANVAS-CENTER — by-design asymmetry/whitespace), `no-focal` (R-FOCAL-CHECK — by-design N-way parallel/equal-weight comparison with no single hero), `title-gap` (R-VIS-TITLE-GAP — bespoke header-less title+subtitle spacing); plus `no-iframe` (R-DEMO-IFRAME, checked deck.json-side by validate-deck.py — explicitly accept that a page originally imported as an iframe-embed live demo has been deliberately rebuilt without its <iframe>). Use ONLY when the design is genuinely intentional; it is a documented exception, not a silence button. Element-level opt-outs (data-allow-body-floor / typescale / drop-shadow / peer-size …) are authored inline in `data.html`, NOT here.

language_override.description: Override deck.language for this slide only.

custom_css.description: Per-slide CSS, authored WITHOUT a scope prefix. The renderer wraps each selector in `.slide[data-slide-key=KEY]` and emits it as a co-located `<style data-fs-custom-css>` block inside the slide, so the CSS travels with the slide on lift/clone/paste and round-trips (never a head/page `<style>` that vanishes on republish). Already `[data-slide-key=]`-scoped selectors pass through; legacy `[data-page=NN]` selectors are rewritten to the slide-key scope; `@media`/`@supports` recurse; `@keyframes`/`@font-face` pass verbatim. Use sparingly — still subject to R20/R06 type-ladder rules.

notes.description: Presenter notes / authoring intent. Not rendered.

title_style.description: Per-slide override of deck.title_style. See deck_meta.title_style.

logo_position.description: Per-slide override of deck.logo_position. See deck_meta.logo_position.

hidden.description: 隐藏页 (PPT-style 'hide slide'). The slide IS still rendered (gets data-hidden) and stays reachable by a direct #N/#key hash and in scroll mode, but the runtime SKIPS it in linear present-mode navigation (→/←/space/wheel/swipe/prev-next) and excludes it from the page count. For appendix/backup/draft/'skip for this audience' slides you want to keep but not walk through. Durable — re-render/publish keep it. Toggle with `deck-cli hide/unhide KEY`. Distinct from `_disabled` (which means 'this slide errors' and is dropped from render entirely).

_disabled.description: If true, renderer skips this slide entirely (not rendered at all). Escape hatch for 'this slide errors, let the rest render'. SKILL.md Troubleshooting section. To keep a slide in the deck but skip it during the show, use the author-facing `hidden` field instead.

_orig_layout.description: Internal: used by `layout: raw` slides migrated from a legacy deck so framework CSS rules engage. Renderer-only; not authored by hand.

lifted.description: Native slide lift marker. Set when a slide is lifted VERBATIM from another deck (value = source ref, e.g. 'opple-ai-lecture#2'). Renderer emits data-lifted; validate.py / visual-audit.js then downgrade this slide's CONTENT-STYLE violations (R06 / R-WHITE-TEXT / R-VIS-BODY-FLOOR / R-VIS-TIER) from error -> WARNING — surfaced so a human chooses to fix, not blocking. GEOMETRY / OVERFLOW rules stay full-severity error (a real overflow is a real failure regardless of lift).

lift_origin.description: Internal: machine-readable lift provenance written by lift-slides.py (F-70: src_deck / src_path / src_key / src_index / lifted_at). Lets heal/re-lift return to the exact source slide. Renderer-ignored; not authored by hand.

data_flow_swim.description: flow/swim — multi-lane roadmap (time axis × product lines). Each lane has its own accent + milestones placed on the time grid. quarter index is 1-based.

items.description: Time period label, e.g. 'Q1' or '2026 H1'.

name.description: Lane name, e.g. '飞书 AI 助手'.

desc.description: Optional sub-text on the milestone tile.

data_arch_stack.description: arch-stack layout — 2-5 horizontal layers (e.g. apps / platform / AI engines / data foundation). Each layer has a name + modules pills.

sub.description: Layer subtitle, e.g. 'APPS · 面向终端用户'.

data_logo_wall.description: logo-wall layout — N industries × M client logos. Logo entries are LOGICAL keys (e.g. '瑞幸咖啡'); enricher resolves to assets/shared/clientlogo/<key>.png.

lede.description: Optional intro paragraph above the industries grid.

name.description: Industry pill label, e.g. '新茶饮 / 餐饮连锁'.

items.description: Logo logical key — corresponds to assets/shared/clientlogo/<key>.png.

data_chart.description: chart layout — deterministic data-viz (variants bar/line/donut) computed from numeric values. Author supplies values only; renderer computes geometry → inline SVG/CSS with brand tokens (no JS). bar/donut use series[0]; line allows 1-3 series for comparison.

series.description: 1 series for bar/donut; 1-3 for line (multi-line comparison).

color.description: Optional series color; else auto-rotate by index.

layout_enum.description: 14 base layouts (content/stats/flow are multi-variant) + 3 specials (replica/raw/canvas). iframe-embed wraps an external HTML page (prototype / live demo / report) as a slide. canvas = absolutely-positioned structured elements (the PPTX → structured-JSON intermediate; see DECKJSON-UNIFIED-INTERMEDIATE-SPEC §3). F-305 «raw unless ceremonial»: every value still RENDERS (legacy decks unaffected), but for NEW pages only the CEREMONIAL set (cover/section/agenda/quote/end) + MECHANISM layouts (raw/canvas/iframe-embed/replica) are recommended; the body-content schemas (content/stats/flow/chart/table/arch-stack/image-text/logo-wall, all variants) are FROZEN — author new body pages as layout:"raw" (advisory R-LAYOUT-DEPRECATED nudges any that aren't; never blocks).

accent_enum.description: Slide accent. cyan deliberately excluded (R49 — cyan is inline-highlight only).

accent.description: Teal-highlighted emphasis word. Single accent per slide (R-LANG family).

icon_ref.description: Icon reference. Either a name from the shipped Lucide-style library, or a raw inline SVG string.

image_ref.description: Image. Path relative to deck file, or absolute, or symbolic 'asset:<key>' resolved against assets manifest.

embeddable_block.description: Composable narrative-pattern block. Embeds inside content (3up/2col).data.body_blocks[].

block_mockup_card.description: UI mockup card · 4 visual kinds (past/now/callout/compare). PPT slide 6. Use inside content/3up.body_blocks or content/blocks to showcase before/after product UI states.

kind.description: past=灰底过去态, now=蓝底现状, callout=带箭头/指示线突出, compare=对比左右两栏

label.description: Top eyebrow label (e.g. '过去' / '现在' / '突出指示').

image.description: Optional UI screenshot path (relative to deck.json dir).

block_persona_card.description: User persona intro · PPT slide 35. name + role + generation + summary.

generation.description: Optional generation tag, e.g. '70 后' / '90 后'.

block_testimonial_card.description: Customer testimonial — name + title + quote + optional portrait + company logo. PPT pattern slide 89-90.

title.description: Job title + company, e.g. 'CIO · XX 集团'.

portrait.description: Optional headshot image src (relative to deck.json dir).

company_logo.description: Optional company logo path/key. If looks like a logical key, resolves to assets/shared/clientlogo/<key>.png; if has slash/extension, used as-is.

block_verdict_grid.description: SKILL.md Pattern B · 2-3 decision-judgment cards in a row. Each card carries a verdict (go/conditional/nogo) which drives its border color + corner badge tone. Cards may inline a kpi_strip-shaped 'kpis' array as supporting evidence.

verdict.description: go=teal, conditional=purple, nogo=orange (per Pattern B color rules).

badge.description: Top label, e.g. '命题 A · 费效真实性' or 'GO · 接'.

body.description: May contain inline accent text via `...` — renderer trusts the HTML inside body.

kpis.description: Optional supporting kpis (same shape as block_kpi_strip.kpis). Inlined here to sidestep the no-block-in-block rule.

block_phone_iframe.description: Phone-frame wrapping an iframe-embedded HTML prototype (the simpler / more reusable of the two phone patterns in SKILL.md 'Embedding prototypes'). The bespoke `.phone` with `.ph-status / .ph-bar / .ph-chat` animated UI is single-deck enough to live in `raw` for now.

iframe_src.description: Relative or absolute URL to the HTML prototype. ALWAYS copy the prototype HTML into the deck's output folder — never reference user-local absolute paths.

hint.description: Optional auto-fade hint pill, e.g. '原型可点击 · Click anywhere'.

data_cover.description: Master spec: title + initiator name + date. No subtitle (opt-in via bilingual setting).

title.description:
Can contain \n for hard line break. Do NOT put a literal
here — the cover title is an ESCAPED field, so a literal
renders escaped and trips R-ESC-HTML at render time. The renderer converts a newline escape into
; deck-cli new-deck auto-normalises a literal
for you.

date.description: Display date, e.g. '2026.05.20' or '客户提案 · 2026 Q2'.

subtitle.description: OPT-IN bilingual EN subtitle. Default is OMIT.

title.description: Optional header. v2 default hides header — pills speak for themselves.

items.description: 1-item agendas are technically allowed (bundle composition can produce them) but editorially weak — consider dropping the agenda for a single-case deck.

title_en.description: Bilingual only. Validator R-LANG ignores agenda title_en even in zh-only — common pattern.

active.description: Recap variant — highlight one item. Multiple actives invalid.

dim.description: Recap variant — explicitly dim a non-active item.

chapter_num.description: E.g. '02.' (top-level) or '02.1.' (subsection · PPT layout 3 '二级章节页'). Trailing period required per master spec.

parent_label.description: Optional parent-chapter label, e.g. '02. 业务挑战 > 02.1 客户视角'. Shown above the title when this is a subsection.

pills.description: Ghost pills for related products. Optional.

data_content_3up.description: content layout · variant 3up — 3 parallel pillar cards. With optional lede above + per-card kpi, this variant absorbs the exec-summary use case (thesis + 3 takeaways with KPIs + closing CTA via body_blocks[cta-box]).

title.description:
Single-line. R13: no
in content-page title. May contain inline ... highlights.

lede.description: Optional thesis / intro paragraph above the 3-up cards. Used for exec-summary-style slides.

footer_label.description: Small Latin tag at card foot, e.g. 'MESSENGER · DOCS'.

kpi.description: Optional per-card KPI badge. Used for exec-summary takeaways (each takeaway shows a hero number like '28/30', '+44%', '96%').

body_blocks.description: Optional richness blocks appended below the 3-up grid (e.g. cta-box for exec-summary actions, pullquote for emphasis).

data_content_matrix.description: content layout · variant matrix — 2×2 strategic prioritization grid with named axes and 4 quadrants. Classic consulting pattern (Eisenhower, Impact vs Effort, etc.). Per Pattern-B-style color cues: TL=teal (act-now/quick-win), TR=blue (strategic), BL=muted (defer), BR=orange (drop).

quadrants.description: All 4 quadrants required. tl=top-left, tr=top-right, bl=bottom-left, br=bottom-right (CSS quad positions).

ord.description: Optional ordinal letter (A/B/C/D). Renderer can also auto-assign.

title.description: Quadrant headline, e.g. '立即做 · 快赢'.

data_content_2col.description: content layout · variant 2col — narrative text + supporting visual.

svg.description: Raw inline <svg>…</svg>. Validator skips its hex palette per R10.

data_content_blocks.description: content layout · variant blocks — full-width body_blocks page (no text+visual split). Used when the slide body is one or more narrative-pattern blocks (verdict-grid, kpi-strip, etc.) occupying the full canvas width. Renderer typically emits `data-layout="content-2col"` with a single full-width child grid.

title.description: May contain inline highlight HTML, e.g. `两件事` — renderer trusts the HTML inside title for this variant.

source_footer.description: Inline data citation, e.g. '数据来源 · 行业访谈 N=24'. Optional.

data_content_before_after.description: content layout · variant before-after — two-column comparison (痛点 vs 飞书后), with a center pivot. Symmetry required: before.items.length === after.items.length.

tag.description: Left-column header label, e.g. '现状 · 痛点'.

caption.description: Center-column caption, e.g. '用飞书 12 周后'.

tag.description: Right-column header label, e.g. '飞书 · 现在'.

data_content_story_case.description: content layout · variant story-case — one-pager customer case (the existing Path A pattern, now folded under content).

source.description: Optional. NEVER fabricate '客户访谈' placeholders.

data_quote.description: Single quote slide. accent_phrase splits lead/accent/tail so teal highlight is byte-perfect.

title.description: For screen_label / texts.md. Not visually rendered (the quote IS the page).

data_stats_row.description: stats layout · variant row — 3-4 KPIs in a horizontal strip.

trend.description: E.g. '↑ 触达' / '↓ 决策'. Optional.

footnote.description: Slide-level disclosure of sample / methodology. Optional.

data_stats_waterfall.description: stats layout · variant waterfall — 桥图. Baseline value + N positive/negative contributions + final value. Renderer auto-scales bar heights from max(value); authors don't specify pixels. Connectors between bar tops drawn automatically.

bars.description: Ordered bars from left to right. Must start with `base` and end with `end`. Middle bars are `pos` (positive contribution) or `neg` (negative).

kind.description: base=起点, pos=正向贡献, neg=负向, end=终点合计.

delta.description: Secondary label, e.g. '+ 29%' / '起点' / '合计 + 44%'. Optional.

label.description: Bar primary label, e.g. '渠道扩展'.

sublabel.description: Bar sub-label, e.g. '新签 12 家头部'. Optional.

data_stats_hero.description: stats layout · variant hero — 1 big number + supporting prose.

title.description: For screen_label. Optional — falls back to heading.

eyebrow.description: Small accent label above the number (optional).

heading.description: One-line takeaway — meaning of the number.

body.description: Method / scope / context of the number.

items.description: Cell count MUST match headers.length. Validator enforces.

data_flow_timeline.description: flow layout · variant timeline — 3-6 chronological milestones along an axis.

data_flow_tree.description: flow layout · variant tree — MECE issue-tree (根因拆解 / 问题分解). Root question + 2-4 first-level branches, each branch with 1-4 leaves. Renderer draws SVG dashed connectors root→branches and branch→leaves automatically.

question.description:
Root question, e.g. '为什么 ROI 未达预期?'. May contain
for two-line display.

why.description: Optional supporting context, e.g. '实际 1.8× · 预期 3.0×'. May contain inline ... for emphasis.

branches.description: MECE branches — 2 minimum (binary split is the simplest valid decomposition), 4 maximum (visual density limit).

ord.description: Optional ordinal (A/B/C/D or 01/02/03). Renderer can also auto-assign.

title.description: Branch headline, e.g. '客户深度使用率低'.

data_flow_process.description: flow layout · variant process — 3-6 sequential workflow steps with chevrons.

num.description: Step numeral, e.g. '01'. Optional (auto-numbered by renderer).

contact.description: Optional contact line, off-master but allowed.

slogan.description: Optional brand slogan, rendered as big closing line below title. Mirrors PPT '封底(带 slogan)' layout master.

data_replica.description: Replica mode — entire slide is a single page-rendered image. Used for PDF/PPT 1:1 conversion.

page_image.description: Path to the page JPG/PNG. Renderer wraps it as full-bleed background-image.

source_page.description: Original page number in the source PDF (for traceability).

data_raw.description: First-class raw-first default. Per references/deck-generation-policy.md the design stance is raw-first: most content pages are layout:raw. The renderer drops the html string verbatim into the .slide; compose with the framework header, fs- component classes and --fs-* tokens. Universal name-free audits (R-OVERFLOW / R-VIS-* / R10 / R20 …) STILL run on raw — it is NOT an unaudited zone. Fall back to a schema layout only for a pure standard shape (the allowlist table in references/design-first.md is the single source).

data_iframe_embed.description: iframe-embed layout — standard title + 飞书 logo at top, an <iframe> fills the rest of the slide to the bottom. Use for embedding live prototypes, demos, HTML reports, dashboards, or any external HTML page as a slide.

src.description: Path to the embedded HTML, relative to the deck's output directory (e.g. 'prototypes/foo.html').

iframe_title.description: iframe `title` attr for a11y. Defaults to data.title when omitted.

hint.description: Bottom-right pill caption, e.g. '报告可滚动查阅'. Omit / empty to hide the pill.

zoom.description: Optional content scaling. 1.0 = native (default); >1 enlarges (use when embedded text is hard to read at slide scale); <1 shrinks (use when prototype is wider than the iframe). Implemented via CSS transform + width/height compensation, so the iframe always fills its container regardless of zoom. PREFER `fit_width` for a fixed-width prototype — it derives the right zoom for you.

fit_width.description: PREFERRED for embedding a fixed-width foreign prototype/H5. Set to the prototype's DESIGN width in px (its `max-width` / container width, e.g. 1320). The renderer derives `zoom` = 1800 / fit_width so the prototype renders AT its design width then scales up to fill the ~1800px embed body — bigger text, no empty side gaps. Fills by width; a taller-than-frame app stays scrollable. Ignored if `zoom` is set explicitly. Do NOT resize the iframe via custom_css !important (framework rule out-specifies it → clips/de-centers).

data_canvas.description: canvas layout — a slide as a structured list of absolutely-positioned elements (text boxes / images / shapes), NOT an HTML blob and NOT an image. This is the PPTX → structured-JSON intermediate (DECKJSON-UNIFIED-INTERMEDIATE-SPEC §3). Geometry is px on a canvas_w × canvas_h plane (default 1920×1080); the renderer converts to cqw/cqh. Each element carries a stable `id` that is the round-trip anchor for sync-index-to-deck.py. placeholder:true marks a page that couldn't be reconstructed (live chart / SmartArt / OLE) — rendered as a '本页待重做 · 源第 N 页' notice.

canvas_w.description: Canvas width in px the element geometry is expressed against.

canvas_h.description: Canvas height in px the element geometry is expressed against.

placeholder.description: If true the page could not be reconstructed; renderer shows a '待重做' notice instead of elements.

source_page.description: Original page number in the source PPTX/PDF (for traceability + the placeholder notice).

title.description: Optional framework chrome: when present the renderer emits the standard wordmark + .header > h2.title-zh above the canvas plane (master title coords), so an imported page adopts the deck's native title instead of its baked-in PPTX heading. Remove the imported heading elements from `elements` when using this.

subtitle.description: Optional .page-sub line under the chrome title (requires `title`).

elements.description: Absolutely-positioned elements. Empty allowed when placeholder:true.

id.description: Stable element id; the by-id round-trip anchor (rendered as data-el-id). Must be unique within the slide.

anchor.description: Vertical text anchoring within the box. Optional.

align.description: type=text only: horizontal paragraph alignment → text wrapper text-align. Optional.

insets.description: Text box padding [left, right, top, bottom] px. Optional.

runs.description: type=text only: inline runs. Multi-run preserves per-run bold/color; editing the box's text in the browser may flatten to a single run (documented lossy boundary).

color.description: CSS color, e.g. '#1A1A1A'. For a gradient run this is the flat first-stop fallback.

size.description: Font size in px on canvas.

grad.description: Optional CSS gradient for gradient TEXT (rendered via background-clip:text); e.g. 'linear-gradient(45deg,#FFF 0%,#F7BE86 100%)'.

font.description: Optional CSS font-family list from the run's real PPTX typeface (latin then ea), e.g. '"TikTok Display", "FZLanTingHeiPro_GB18030"'.

src.description: type=image only: real asset path (e.g. 'input/p1.jpg'). Kept verbatim so copy-assets / lift scan + copy it.

crop.description: type=image only: OOXML srcRect crop [l,r,t,b] as fractions (may be negative). Visible region x∈[l,1-r], y∈[t,1-b] is stretched to the box.

fill.description: type=shape only: solid background CSS color (e.g. '#1A73E8').

kind.description: type=shape only: OOXML preset geometry name (e.g. 'rect','roundRect','ellipse'). Hint for the renderer; informational.

gradient.description: type=shape only: a full CSS gradient value (e.g. 'linear-gradient(135deg, #A 0%, #B 100%)') applied as background. Used when the fill is a gradient (16% of real shapes).

border.description: type=shape only: outline. color is a CSS color, width is px.

svg.description: type=shape only: an inline SVG body (path/line markup) for FREEFORM/custGeom and LINE shapes the box CSS can't express. Rendered inside an <svg> sized to the element box (viewBox 0 0 100 100, preserveAspectRatio=none).

style.description: type=shape only: a raw CSS string escape-hatch appended to the element style for appearance that doesn't map to fill/gradient/border/radius (e.g. rotation, opacity).
